symptom: MDP templates with duplicate exact keys, underscore/hyphen alias collisions, malformed text lines, or malformed .ndx group-body tokens could be silently reinterpreted before patching and could drive unsafe auto tc-grps decisions.
root_cause: The read path used implicit text decoding and plain dict overwrites, so input validation happened later than write-side cleanup, while .ndx group-size parsing silently ignored non-integer tokens and auto-mode threshold sanitization was duplicated and incomplete for invalid or non-finite values.
trigger: An MDP template contains duplicate exact keys or semantic alias variants, a malformed non-comment line, or an index group body contains non-integer junk while tc-grps auto mode depends on parsed Polymer/NonPolymer sizes.
fix: Use one explicit UTF-8 MDP read path, normalize duplicate exact and semantic keys during parse with strict/non-strict diagnostics, preserve malformed-line strict failures, reject malformed .ndx group-body tokens, and centralize tc-grps threshold sanitization before auto/split decisions.
prevention: Use one parser/decoder path for both template reads and post-write validation.
Detect duplicate exact keys and semantic alias collisions before patching, not only during final write cleanup.
Treat malformed .ndx group-body tokens as unsafe whenever thermostat decisions depend on parsed group sizes.
Sanitize invalid or non-finite safety thresholds in one helper before comparing group sizes/fractions.
notes: DiagnosticSink was left unchanged because the current code did not expose a concrete same-instance cross-phase contamination bug.
